relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Mary Magdalene comes in darkness and sees the stone removed.
She runs to Peter and the beloved disciple with the missing-body grief.
The two disciples go toward the tomb.
The beloved disciple reaches the tomb first.
He sees the linen but waits outside.
Peter enters and beholds the linen cloths.
The face cloth lies folded apart.
The beloved disciple enters, sees, and trusts.
Scripture-understanding is still opening.
The disciples return to their own.
Mary remains weeping at the tomb.
Two white messengers sit where the body had lain.
They ask why she weeps; she seeks her Lord.
Jesus stands near, unrecognized.
She mistakes him for the gardener while seeking the body.
One spoken name opens recognition: Mary.
She is sent to announce ascension to my Father and your Father.
Mary Magdalene becomes apostolic witness: I have seen the Lord.
Behind locked doors of fear, Jesus stands with peace.
Hands and side turn fear into joy.
Peace becomes sending as the Father sent him.
He breathes holy Spirit into them.
Release and holding of misalignment are entrusted to the community.
Thomas is absent from the first peace-appearance.
He asks for touchable wounds before trust.
Eight days later, peace enters locked doors again.
Thomas is invited into the wounds and into trust.
Thomas answers with the Gospel’s high confession: my Lord and my Divine.
Blessing opens for those trusting without seeing.
Many signs remain unwritten.
These are written so trust may become life in his name.
